{{Quote|Use your harpoon and tow cables...go for their legs! It's our only hope of stopping them!|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 40">[[Star Wars (1977) 40|''Star Wars'' (1977) 40]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 40: The Empire Strikes Back: Battleground Hoth''''' is the fortieth issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
At the medical center of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] is recovering from wounds sustained from an attack by a [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] ice monster. A tender moment between [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and him is briefly shared before [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] enter to check on Luke's status. Leia and Han argue a bit and Leia kisses Luke in order to make Han jealous.

Meanwhile on board the super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Lord Vader's]] crew receives the information transmitted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]]. Vader knows that the rebel base is on Hoth and orders the fleet towards that location.

Admiral [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Ozzel]] of the Empire brings the fleet out of [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]] too quickly thus alerting the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] to their presence. As such, the rebels activate an energy field keeping all laser fire from reaching the planet. As punishment for this, Darth Vader uses [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to strangle Ozzel. He promotes Captain [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Piett]] of the ''Executor'' to Ozzel's position.

The Empire deploys [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers to the planets surface. Under the command of General [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Veers]], their purpose is to take out the generators that power the energy field. Luke Skywalker leads a squad of [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]] to counter the Walkers while [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]] leads the ground assault.

Han Solo meanwhile, has [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] prep the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] so that he can get the Princess off the planet.

During the ground battle, Luke's gunner [[Dak Ralter/Legends|Dak Ralter]] is killed and their speeder crashes into a snow bank. An Imperial Walker bears down on them ready to crush the crippled speeder with one of its feet.

==Continuity==
This is Chapter Two of the six-part adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

In the original shooting script, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] had captured several [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] monsters and imprisoned them at [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]]. The scenes never appeared in the final drafts however and were not included in the movie. The [[Marvel Comics]] adaptation re-inserts references to the wampas being held captive at the base.

Artist [[Al Williamson]] incorrectly switches the roles of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Ozzel]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Piett]] in this issue.
